ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (Mango Sandstorm / MERCURY) RMM deployment via phishing-delivered MSI (ScreenConnect, Atera, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp, Syncro)
ucs.description: Hunts MuddyWater's signature initial-access-to-foothold chain since 2021: spear-phishing lure (Office/PDF/HTML link) → user double-click → MSI installer of a legitimate RMM (overwhelmingly ConnectWise ScreenConnect, also Atera, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp, Syncro, Action1) which the operator then uses for hands-on-keyboard activity. This combination — Iranian state targeting (Israeli, Gulf, US gov/telco) + commodity RMM-as-RAT — is a publicly attributed MuddyWater hallmark (Microsoft DEV-1084 / MERCURY, CISA AA22-055A, Mandiant TEMP.Zagros).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe") AND (Processes.process_name IN ("msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe") OR Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","Action1_agent.exe","AnyDesk.exe","MeshAgent.exe")) AND (Processes.process IN ("*screenconnect*","*ConnectWiseControl*","*atera*","*remoteutilities*","*rutserv*","*simple-help*","*SimpleHelpCustomer*","*syncro*","*anydesk*","*meshagent*","*action1*","*goto-resolve*")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left host [ | tstats summariesonly=t count as net_count values(All_Traffic.dest) as rmm_dest from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest IN ("*.screenconnect.com","*.connectwisecontrol.com","*.atera.com","*.remoteutilities.net","*.simple-help.com","*.syncromsp.com","*.action1.com","*.anydesk.com")) by All_Traffic.src_host | rename All_Traffic.src_host as host ] | join type=left user [ search index=email sourcetype IN ("ms:o365:reporting:messagetrace","o365:management:activity","mscs:azure:eventhub:email") earliest=-7d@d | eval user=lower(recipient) | stats min(_time) as email_time values(sender) as sender values(subject) as subject by user ] | where isnotnull(net_count) OR isnotnull(email_time) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | `security_content_ctime(email_time)`
ucs.kql:
let rmm_strings = dynamic(["screenconnect","connectwisecontrol","atera","remoteutilities","rutserv","rfusclient","simple-help","simplehelpcustomer","syncro","anydesk","meshagent","action1","goto-resolve"]);
let rmm_procs = dynamic(["ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","Action1_agent.exe","AnyDesk.exe","MeshAgent.exe"]);
let lure_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let rmm_install = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (lure_parents) and (FileName in~ ("msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any (rmm_strings)))
   or  (FileName in~ (rmm_procs))
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine;
let rmm_net = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("screenconnect.com","connectwisecontrol.com","atera.com","remoteutilities.net","simple-help.com","syncromsp.com","action1.com","anydesk.com","goto-resolve.com")
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let phish = EmailEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where Subject has_any ("invoice","ministry","tender","salary","memo","circular","israel","municipality","foreign affairs")
   or SenderFromDomain has_any (".onmicrosoft.com",".ir")
| project EmailTime=Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, NetworkMessageId;
rmm_install
| join kind=leftouter (rmm_net) on DeviceId
| join kind=leftouter (phish) on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl) or isnotempty(EmailTime)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, SenderFromAddress, Subject
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater is the most prolific abuser of legitimate RMM tooling among nation-state actors — Microsoft (MERCURY/Mango Sandstorm), CISA (AA22-055A), and Mandiant have all published that ScreenConnect, Atera, RemoteUtilities and SimpleHelp MSIs delivered via phishing are this group's primary post-2021 intrusion vector. Joining the email lure → office/browser parent → msiexec installing a remote-mana

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater PowGoop loader: goopdate.dll side-loaded by relocated GoogleUpdate.exe
ucs.description: Detects the PowGoop / POWERSTATS-NG loader: MuddyWater drops a legitimately signed GoogleUpdate.exe outside its normal install path together with a malicious goopdate.dll that decrypts a goopdate.dat PowerShell stage. Catching GoogleUpdate.exe loading goopdate.dll from anywhere other than Program Files\Google\Update is essentially actor-pathognomonic for MuddyWater (CISA AA22-055A, Trend Micro 'Earth Vetala', PAN Unit 42 PowGoop reporting).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="GoogleUpdate.exe" AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Update\\*","C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Update\\*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=t count as fs_hits values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("goopdate.dll","goopdate.dat","config.txt","libcurl.dll","libcurlpp.dll") AND NOT (Filesystem.file_path IN ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Update\\*","C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Update\\*")) by Filesystem.dest | rename Filesystem.dest as host ] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let legit_paths = dynamic([@"c:\program files (x86)\google\update\", @"c:\program files\google\update\"]);
let sideload = DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "GoogleUpdate.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("goopdate.dll","goopdate.dat","libcurl.dll","libcurlpp.dll")
| extend lower_init = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where not(lower_init startswith legit_paths[0]) and not(lower_init startswith legit_paths[1])
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let drops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ ("goopdate.dll","goopdate.dat","GoogleUpdate.exe","config.txt")
| extend lower_path = tolower(FolderPath)
| where not(lower_path startswith legit_paths[0]) and not(lower_path startswith legit_paths[1])
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedFile=FileName, DropFolder=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
sideload
| join kind=leftouter (drops) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, FileName, SHA256, DropTime, DroppedFile, DropFolder, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine1
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: PowGoop is a MuddyWater-exclusive loader family: every public report (CISA AA22-055A, PAN Unit 42, Deep Instinct, ClearSky) attributes goopdate.dll side-loading by a relocated GoogleUpdate.exe to MuddyWater/MERCURY operations against Israel and Gulf states. The legitimate Google updater never runs from %TEMP%, %APPDATA%, %PROGRAMDATA% or user profile paths, so a GoogleUpdate.exe outside Program Fi
